_schema.description: Gaussian mixture parameters for multimodal distribution visualisation. Each component defines one mode (peak). The chart generates synthetic samples from the mixture and renders a KDE curve, histogram, and summary statistics. To show a real dataset, replace 'components' with your actual sample values in the 'samples' array and set 'useRealSamples': true.

fields.components: array — each entry defines one Gaussian component: { mean, sd, weight }. Weights should sum to 1.
fields.n: number — number of synthetic samples to draw from the mixture
fields.bandwidth: number — KDE bandwidth (Silverman rule used if omitted)
fields.useRealSamples: boolean — if true, use 'samples' array directly instead of generating from components
fields.samples: array of numbers (optional) — real data values; used when useRealSamples is true

_schema.replace: Adjust mean, sd, and weight of each component to match your real mixture. Add a third component for trimodal. Set useRealSamples: true and populate samples[] to visualise real data directly.
